title: Cohort Definitions
description: Cohort Definitions is the foundational module for specifying patient populations used across all analytical workflows in Parthenon. Using a structured, criteria-based interface aligned with OHDSI standards, researchers can define entry events, inclusion rules, cohort exit strategies, and then generate cohort counts across one or more data sources.
tips: Anchor your cohort on a well-defined index event — such as a first diagnosis code or medication dispensing — to ensure a consistent observation start date across all patients.
Attach concept sets to inclusion criteria rather than hard-coding individual codes so that your definition remains maintainable as vocabularies are updated.
Use the cohort generation report to review attrition at each inclusion rule step and identify criteria that may be overly restrictive for your target population.
Version your cohort definitions before making changes so that prior analytical runs remain reproducible and traceable.
Share cohort definitions with collaborators via the Phenotype Library to promote reuse and harmonization across sites.
